topic: Evaluating region-level deconfounded routing versus global similarity gating for reducing multi-concept MRSA contamination under shared-context conflicts

hypotheses.name: Local deconfounded routing outperforms global similarity gating on hardest compositions
hypotheses.claim: For multi-concept MRSA, region-level conflict routing using foreground-focused, deconfounded similarity should outperform raw global similarity gating, especially on matched-background or shared-context prompts.
hypotheses.predicted_outcome: Relative to vanilla MRSA and global similarity-only gating, the method was expected to reduce region-level contamination by at least 5% on high-conflict prompts and improve per-concept identity/reference alignment on shared-context subsets with minimal loss elsewhere.

hypotheses.name: Foreground-only similarity without conflict routing is insufficient
hypotheses.claim: Using only foreground-masked similarity as a global weight should help somewhat over full-image similarity but underperform a method that also includes contested-region routing and explicit background deconfounding.

hypotheses.name: Conflict routing without deconfounding is insufficient on shared-context cases
hypotheses.claim: Local routing alone should reduce some interference, but using full-image similarity instead of foreground-minus-background deconfounded similarity should remain vulnerable to shared-context confounds.

hypotheses.name: Temporal scheduling of exclusivity matters
hypotheses.claim: A three-phase denoising schedule with stronger early separation should outperform a static exclusivity setting by better balancing early concept separation and later detail integration.

hypotheses.name: Early ownership stabilization matters more than late-only separation
hypotheses.claim: Applying stronger exclusivity only in late denoising steps should be less effective than early intervention for reducing identity flips and improving fidelity.

method: The experiment evaluated a proposed training-free MRSA control strategy that combines foreground-focused deconfounded similarity, contested-region routing, and scheduling across denoising steps. It compared the full method against vanilla MRSA and mechanism-targeted ablations isolating foreground similarity, background deconfounding, local routing, and temporal scheduling. Analysis emphasized the matched-background/shared-context slice as the main stress test and used region_level_contamination_error as the primary metric.

settings.model: MRSA-based multi-concept generation system; exact backbone not clearly reported
settings.dataset: Multi-concept benchmark with matched-background/shared-context and clean/mismatched-context subsets; exact dataset name not clearly reported

run_quality.analysis_quality_note: Exploratory only; metric direction and narrative appear inconsistent

conclusions: As reported, the proposed ForegroundDeconfoundedConflictRoutedMRSA does not outperform vanilla MRSA on the primary metric.
Under the stated metric direction of lower-is-better, the full proposed method appears worse than vanilla overall, worse on the matched/shared-context stress slice, and worse on the clean subset.
The current experiment provides exploratory rather than confirmatory evidence because reporting is effectively n=1 per cell despite 5 seeds being noted.
The matched/shared-context slice remains the correct and most informative benchmark for this research question.
The ablation structure is scientifically useful because it targets similarity choice, deconfounding, routing, and temporal scheduling separately.
A central comparison needed to test the main claim, GlobalSimilarityWeightedMRSA, is missing or inconsistently reported, preventing the intended hypothesis test.
insights: Global similarity may be the wrong abstraction for MRSA failures because interference is often local and tied to nuisance context rather than whole-image semantics.

Background or shared-context similarity is a plausible confound that can misroute attention, so separating foreground evidence from background agreement remains a promising idea even though it was not validated here.
The strongest stress test is not overall average performance but matched-background/shared-context compositions where leakage pressure is highest.
Mechanism-oriented ablations are valuable: they can reveal whether gains come from local routing, deconfounding, or temporal scheduling rather than from an undifferentiated full method.
Reporting integrity can dominate scientific interpretation; a sign error or metric-direction mismatch can invert the conclusion of the study.
limitations: Run status was failed, so the experiment is incomplete.
Although 5 seeds were noted, results were effectively reported as n=1 per condition cell, making variance and reliability unclear.
The key baseline GlobalSimilarityWeightedMRSA was missing or inconsistently reported, undermining the core comparison.
There is a serious inconsistency between the stated metric direction and the narrative interpretation of the results.
Exact model backbone, dataset identity, hyperparameter values, and hardware details were not clearly reported.
Because only partial quantitative outputs were available, several ablations could not be conclusively interpreted.
suggested_directions: Rerun the study with complete and valid logging, full seed aggregation, and explicit per-seed reporting for all registered conditions.
Resolve metric-definition and table-sign consistency before drawing any scientific conclusion.
Include and clearly report the missing GlobalSimilarityWeightedMRSA baseline, since it is essential to testing the main hypothesis.
Evaluate the proposed method primarily on matched-background/shared-context cases, while also reporting clean-slice trade-offs separately.
Add direct diagnostics for foreground similarity, background similarity, and disagreement-triggered routing to verify whether deconfounding activates as intended.
Perform localized analyses of contamination in contested regions instead of relying only on global averages.
Test whether temporal scheduling and early-step interventions matter independently by running full factorial ablations with sufficient replication.
If the deconfounded routing idea still looks promising after fixing reporting issues, refine the gating logic rather than pivoting away from the core premise.
research_topic: [World Model] Adaptive multi-reference self-attention (MRSA) for FreeCustom: dynamically scaling attention weights based on concept similarity to improve multi-concept composition quality
